title: Bot character stored in global.* instead of 2.0 storage.*
description: Finding 3: src/client/lua.rs:336-337,356 create/read global.factorioctl_character while blueprint/chat code uses storage.* (lua.rs:1557,1576,1594,1615,1715,1732). Split-brain persistence; unreliable/dead headless. Migrate bot char handle to storage.*.

title: Character lookup prioritizes connected human (puppets the player)
description: Finding 2: ~16 sites in src/client/lua.rs (379,401,431,457,489,548,567,589,690,757,791,810,925,997,1913,2760) + ~9 in src/client/mod.rs pick game.connected_players[1].character before the bot char. With a human online, place/craft/walk/mine/clear operate on the HUMAN body. Make lookup agent_id-scoped via storage.characters; dedupe into one helper.

title: Separate AI body: agent-scoped character & entity binding
description: Headline fix. Replace copy-pasted character lookup (loop game.connected_players -> first human, else global.factorioctl_character) with agent_id-scoped lookup into the mod storage.characters. Dedupe ~25 inline copies into one helper. Unifies Findings 2,3,4,8. Outcome: agent drives ITS body; human keeps theirs.
notes:
Choir spec: .choir/context/agent-scoped-character-binding-spec.md
Choir filed PR #3
Choir notify_parent: PR filed for factorioctl-upj.1: https://github.com/brickfrog/factorioctl/pull/3. Implemented AgentId plumbing, exact resolver helpers, agent-scoped character storage, registry-first entity binding, init --x/--y behavior, static/golden tests, and smoke_agent_binding.sh. Verified cargo test and bash -n scripts/smoke_agent_binding.sh; live smoke not run here because no reachable headless Factorio/RCON server.

title: Golden-test harness for generated Lua strings
description: Finding 15: src/client/lua.rs has ZERO tests for the Lua it emits; MCP binary has 0 tests. Build snapshot/golden tests over generated Lua: assert escaping, assert no same-line trailing comments, assert get_contents new-format shape, assert error-JSON shape. Blocks the risky Lua fixes so they land with coverage.

I did not fake a red check. Need TL/gate reset or instruction for supported refresh path.

title: North star: LLM teammate that plays Factorio multiplayer alongside a human
description: Umbrella goal: an LLM agent that joins our multiplayer game as its OWN body (not puppeting the human), can chat in-game, take legible actions, and not fall over on a real-sized factory. Child features track the capability gaps; audit epic tracks the engine rot underneath.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI (cargo test) so green-CI satisfies the merge verify gate
description: No CI on the fork meant verify_satisfied_by_ci=false forced per-push local TDD attestation, which deadlocks after a post-review fix push (terminal TDD phase, no honest re-attest). Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running cargo build/test on push+PR so future waves fork with verify_satisfied_by_ci=true and green CI satisfies the gate.

title: Systemic Lua injection via format!() string interpolation
description: Finding 5: entity/recipe/blueprint args interpolated into Lua with raw double quotes across dozens of sites (lua.rs:38,163,231,702,767,819+, mod.rs:512,709,1060+); reachable from CLI (cli/place.rs, craft.rs, insert.rs, extract.rs, set_recipe.rs) and MCP (mcp.rs:1239,1270,1285,1300,1321). A quote/backslash/newline breaks syntax or runs arbitrary Lua over RCON. Add a proper Lua-literal escaper.

title: execute_lua MCP tool is unauthenticated arbitrary-Lua foot-cannon
description: Finding 6: src/bin/mcp.rs:1758-1768 exposes execute_lua running arbitrary Lua via RCON with no allowlist/gate/read-only/confirmation/audit-log. The MCP surface is for LLM agents, so prompt-injected Lua executes freely. Gate behind an env/flag and/or remove from default tool set.

title: get_contents() parsed in old dict format (2.0 returns object array)
description: Finding 1: src/client/lua.rs:141-145 (get_entity_inventory) iterates old {[name]=count} format; also lua.rs:2710,2718 and mod.rs:1440 for transport-line contents. 2.0 returns array of {name,count,quality} -> numeric names + table counts = JSON garbage. New format used correctly elsewhere (lua.rs:467,601,665,736,1921,...).

title: RCON reads a single packet, no ID/type validation
description: Finding 12: src/client/rcon.rs:115-169 returns the next packet body with no request_id/packet_type/terminator handling; unused SERVERDATA_* consts (rcon.rs:13-15). Source RCON fragments long responses -> truncated JSON -> random serde failures. Implement multi-packet reassembly.
notes:
Choir notify_parent: Implemented and committed RCON multi-packet reassembly (commit 82ba213, cargo test green, branch ahead main by 1), but file_pr failed after clean retry: GraphQL reported Head sha can't be blank, Base sha can't be blank, No commits between main and main.audit-completion-protocol-leaf-you-1782586060182-435903-1, Head ref must be a branch.

title: Chat capture registers on_console_chat from RCON, errors discarded
description: Finding 11: src/client/lua.rs:1732-1748 registers script.on_event from an RCON command; src/bin/mcp.rs:578-584 runs it every call and drops errors (let _ = ...). If command-context registration isn't persistent, player chat silently never works.

title: Mutating actions swallow Lua error JSON and report success
description: Finding 7: src/client/mod.rs:666,701,642,359,367 discard Lua {"error":...} from insert_items/set_recipe/remove_entity/teleport/walk; MCP (mcp.rs:1285,1321,1337) and CLI announce success anyway. Propagate real outcomes.

title: extract_items deposits into game.players[1], not the acting character
description: Finding 8: src/client/lua.rs:1195-1203 hard-codes game.players[1] for the destination inventory; wrong in MP, fails headless ('No player character found'). MCP desc claims 'into character inventory' (mcp.rs:1292). Use the same agent-scoped character.

title: Entity-by-unit_number is a hardcoded +/-500 area scan
description: Finding 4: src/client/lua.rs:78,110,1068,1093,1134,1178,1241 implement get-entity-by-id as find_entities_filtered over a 1000x1000 box. Entities outside +/-500 are 'not found'; every insert/extract/remove/rotate/set_recipe is O(area). Use a real registry/reliable lookup.

title: Trustworthy action feedback (no false success)
description: A teammate must know when actions fail. Mutating ops swallow Lua error JSON and report success (Finding 7). Surface real outcomes via CLI + MCP.

title: Robust RCON transport for large factories
description: Large Lua JSON responses fragment across packets; client reads one packet, no ID/type validation (Finding 12) -> random parse failures as the factory grows.

title: Reliable in-game chat loop
description: Agent must reliably receive player chat and reply. on_console_chat handler is registered from an RCON command with errors discarded (Finding 11).

title: factorioctl audit remediation (HEAD c3abe9e, Sarcasmotron, 15 findings)
description: Remediation epic for the code audit of the forked factorioctl engine. Receipt: .choir/state/audit-receipts/2567864b88b5.json. Live-game logic lives in Lua string literals the Rust compiler never checks.

title: Blueprint save/restore round-trips slot-1 by name only (data loss)
description: Finding 13: lua.rs:1490,1540,1634,1677 save 'slot.name', overwrite slot 1 with a blueprint, then set_stack{name=saved}. Drops count/quality/durability/filters/modules/books as a side effect of export. Save and restore the full stack, or use a scratch inventory.

title: ServerManager only tracks processes it started (status/stop theater)
description: Finding 14: src/client/server.rs:11-12 keeps server_process in memory; status/stop (server.rs:120-135) only see a child this instance spawned. A per-invocation CLI can't see a server started earlier; tests fall back to pgrep (tests/setup.sh:67). Track via pidfile/port probe.

title: Inline-Lua-comment footgun live and violated at mcp.rs:684
description: Finding 9: CLAUDE.md:372-384 forbids same-line trailing Lua comments because the executor (mod.rs:56-73) joins lines with spaces and only strips lines starting with --. src/bin/mcp.rs:684 has 'local belt_dir = dir  -- comment' which eats the rest of the joined line; can corrupt get_machine_belt_positions. Strip/forbid in code, not vibes.

title: MCP uses get_entity_by_unit_number that core says doesn't work
description: Finding 10: src/bin/mcp.rs:676-679 uses game.get_entity_by_unit_number for drills, but src/client/lua.rs:1132 explicitly notes it 'doesn't work for Lua-created entities' and uses a scan. The 'ALWAYS use this tool' path (mcp.rs:654) can fail for the very entities this engine creates.
